THE CITY OF NEW YORK VS. HOMER SIMPSON by Tan Maxtone-Graham ACT ONE FADE IN: SCENE 1 INT. MOE’S TAVERN - DAY

place is empty. MOE looks at his watch. It reads 4:59

MOE Uh-oh. Here comes the evening rush. Clear out, fellas. Moe BANGS on the floor with a broom handle. Immediately, dozens of RATS emerge from every nook and cranny in the bar and stream out through a small swinging rat-door. A distant factory whistle BLOWS, and two seconds later, HOMER, LENNY, CARL, SMITTY and a couple other BARFLIES pile in. hos BARFLIES What a day. / Let’s get started. / ‘Evening, Moe. / (ETC.)

BARNEY emerges from the men’s room wearing a bathrobe and slippers and BRUSHING his teeth.

BARNEY ‘Morning, Moe.

MOE Listen up, guys. The Springfield Police have told me that 91 per cent of all traffic accidents are caused by you

six guys.

RECORD 11/4/96

> Pa] bo bo

BARFLIES SCENE 1 (CONT’D) (PROUD MURMURS AND BACKSLAPPING) MOE I know, I know. But the bad news is we gotta start having designated drivers. BARFLIES (UNHAPPY GRUMBLING) MOE We'll choose the same way they pick the Pope. He holds out a large jar. MOE (CONT’D) Everyone reach in and draw a pickled egg. Whoever gets the black egg stays sober tonight. Everyone draws an egg. We PAN ACROSS several people opening their hands and SIGHING with relief. Barney opens his hand to reveal the black egg. BARNEY NO! HOMER (TO BARNEY) You got the black one. DISSOLVE TO: EXT. MOE’S TAVERN - SEVERAL HOURS LATER We hear the sounds of people GETTING ROWDY within. INT. MOE’S TAVERN - CONTINUOUS

Homer, wearing only his underwear, is pretending to fly as several barflies hold him aloft.

Page 2.

HOMER SCENE 1 (CONT’D) Hey everybody, I’m Peter Pantsless!

Barney, sitting at the bar, looks miserable. He’s shaking, sweating and EATING whole unshelled peanuts.

BARNEY (PAINED MOAN) I can’t take this much longer. I gotta have a beer. MOE Relax, you’re in the home stretch. In half an hour you’1ll be safely behind the wheel of a moving vehicle. Just then we hear a DEEP BOOMING BASS RIFF from outside. MUSIC: "OH, YEAH" BY YELLO BARNEY (HORRIFIED) Not tonight. NOT TONIGHT! Through the window, we see the Duff Party Van pulling up outside. (It has lots of adverising all over it and two giant speakers on the sides.) DUFFMAN, a promotional character in a superhero cape and sunglasses, enters the bar, flanked by TWO CHEERLEADERS. (Homer has his pants back on.) DUFFMAN (PARTY VOICE) Are you ready to get Duffed?

The crowd CHEERS as Duffman struts around, throwing them cans of Duff.

LENNY It’s Duffman! The guy in a costume that creates awareness of Duff! HOMER

(A LITTLE TIPSY) We love you, Duffman!

4F22 RECORD

CARL SCENE 1 No, it’s way deeper than love. Don’t listen to him, Duffman.

DUFFMAN Duffman wants to party down with the man who sent in ten thousand Duff labels to bring me here today. I’ve got a bottomless mug of new "Duff Extra

Cold" for... BARNEY GUMBEL!

11/4/96

(CONT! D)

The cheerleaders wheel in a gigantic keg-shaped mug and present it to Barney. Gritting his teeth, he holds onto

the bar with all his strength. CHEERLEADERS Chug! Chug! Chug! Chug! BARNEY (STRAINING) I can’t! I’m the designated driver! The music suddenly SCREECHES to a stop. DUFFMAN (PERFUNCTORY, BRUSQUE) That’s swell. Duff wholeheartedly supports the designated driver program... Now, who wants to party?! CROWD (LOUD STUPID CHEERS)

The MUSIC resumes.

INT. HOMER‘S CAR - LATER THAT NIGHT SCENE 2

Barney drives, bug-eyed with the pressure. Homer, and Carl, very drunk and rowdy, ride with him.

4

4FP22 RECORD 11/4/96

HOMER / LENNY /CARL SCENE 2 (CONT'D (DRUNKENLY SINGING "MACARENA") They SNAP open beers, spray each other, and drink. BARNEY (GRITTED TEETH) Please, I’m trying to drive. LENNY Let’s go to the girls’ college! CARL No, the Playboy Mansion! HOMER It’s my car, and I say we’re going to the lost city of gold! BARNEY That’s just drunk talk. (WISTFUL) Sweet, beautiful drunk talk... EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - A LITTLE LATER

The car pulls up; Homer is the last to be dropped ‘off. He staggers out of the car.

HOMER (TIPSY) Barney. Don’t forget to bring back my car back tomorrow. Just slide it under the door.

BARNEY

(BERILY CALM) Yes. Tomorrow. DISSOLVE TO:

EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - FRONT YARD - DAY SCENE 2 (CONT’D) CHYRON: "TWO MONTHS LATER® Homer is sitting on a folding chair, looking down the road, checking his watch. LISA approaches and hands him a pillow. LISA Here, Dad. Mom sewed you a cushion for your Waitin’ Chair. HOMER Aw, thanks honey. LISA I still don’t understand why you let someone so irresponsible take your car. HOMER It’s not my fault. JI had diminished capacity. _

He waits a beat, then nods as if to say, "It’s true, you know."

EXT. MOE’S TAVERN - AFTERNOON Homer is taping posters to the front of the bar. They read: "Have you seen me?: (picture of Homer’s car) Last seen with: (picture of Barney) ." MOE

Face it, Homer, the car’s gone. It’s

either wrapped around a tree or at the

bottom of the pool at the Chateau

Marmont. Either way -- Barney ain’t

never comin’ back.

A stretch limousine PULLS UP. The CHAUFFEUR gets out and OPENS THE TRUNK. Barney crawls out, holding a bottle.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

BARNEY SCENE 2 (CONT’D)

(TO CHAUFFEUR) Thanks for the lift! The limo DRIVES AWAY.

HOMER Barney, where’s my car?!!

BARNEY All I remember about the last two months is giving a guest lecture at Villanova. (BEAT) Or maybe it was a streetcorner.

HOMER (QUIET ANGER) So you lost my car. That’s just grand. I ought to punch you in the nose, but I have to pick up my kids at school.

Homer gets on a child’s push scooter and pushes himself SQUEAKILY down the street with angry thrusts of his leg.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - GARAGE - EVENING SCENE 3

Homer has laid a mattress out on the floor and placed four tires around it like a car chassis.

MARGE Homer, I don’t want you driving around in a car you built yourself.

HOMER Marge, you can stand there finding fault, or you can knit me some seat

belts.

MARGE SCENE 3 (CONT’D)

Sorry, sorry. She goes back to knitting. Lisa enters with the mail.

LISA (EXCITED) Dad, you got a letter from the City of New York!

HOMER Throw it away! Nothing good has ever come out of New York City!

Lisa opens the letter.

LISA (READING) "Dear motorist, your vehicle is illegally parked in the borough of Manhattan..."

HOMER (HOPEFUL) My vehicle!

LISA "...I£ you do not remedy this malparkage within 72 hours, your car will be thrown into the East River at your expense."

HOMER (WHINY) Oh, I don’t want to go to New York.

MARGE

HOMER SCENE 3 (CONT'D) New York is a hellhole, and you know how I feel about hellholes. LISA Dad, you can’t judge a place you've never been to. BART Yeah! That’s what people do in Russia! HOMER (DEEP BREATH) It’s time I told you about a chapter of my life I hoped would be closed forever. I was on my way to the Harrisburg Coat Outlet to buy an irregular coat. But it required a stdooves in New York City. DISSOLVE TO: EXT. 42ND STREET BETWEEN 8TH AND 7TH - 1970'S - EVENING MUSIC: SCOTT JOPLIN’S "THE ENTERTAINER" 1) A 19-YEAR-OLD HOMER walks along 42nd Street. Porno theater marquees read "The Godfather’s Parts, II," "Jeremiah’s Johnson," and "Five Sleazy Pieces". “HOMER (V.0.) All I had to do was walk a few blocks from the Port Authority Bus Terminal to my train at Penn Station...

2) Homer hands his camera to a STRANGER, who takes his picture very painstakingly, then runs away with the camera.

) A POLICEMAN listens to Homer, then picks up Homer’s uitcase and runs away.

SCENE 3 (CONT’D) 4) Homer eats a hot dog and reads the scrolling news ticker; it reads "Crime Up 8 Million Percent." A PICKPOCKET takes Homer’s wallet. Homer turns around to look and a PIGEON flies off with his hot dog. Three stories above, WOODY ALLEN leans out of a window and empties his garbage can, covering Homer with wet trash. HOMER (V.0O.) Anyhow, I must have looked at somebody the wrong way...

He angrily flings away a piece of the trash, accidentally hitting a nearby SUPERPIMP, who begins chasing after him.

HOMER (V.O. CONT’D) ... because next thing I knew, I was running for my life. Homer flees. He tries to climb a fire escape ladder, but stays in place as the ladder slides down. He reaches the top of the ladder and it slides into ar open manhole with Homer hanging on. BACK TO SCENE HOMER And that’s when the CHUDs came at me. MARGE Oh, Homer. Of course you’ll have a bad impression of New York if you only focus on the pimps and the CHUDs. LISA I’d love to see New York. And we could all go with the bus company’s special

Supersitter Fare!

She holds up a newspaper with a giant print ad that reads "NEW YORK - $9".

BART SCENE 3 (CONT'D) Nine bucks?! This one’s on me. Bart pulls out a wad of cash. MARGE Great! We’re all going to New York! HOMER (GRUMBLE, THEN:) All right, New York, I’m coming back. (TAKES OUT WALLET) But you’re not getting this!

He throws his wallet into the fireplace. It makes a small WHOOSH.

LISA Dad, our baby pictures were in there! HOMER

Don’t you start. FADE OUT:

END_OF ACT ONE

ACT TWO FADE IN: SCENE 4 INT. LINCOLN TUNNEL - A FEW DAYS LATER Through the windows of a bus, we see the Simpsons sleeping. MARGE (0.S.) Wake up, everyone. We’re almost there. INT. BUS - CONTINUOUS

We see Maggie was sleeping in the little net below the luggage rack. Bart and,Lisa YAWN and STRETCH.

BART That took too long! How come we had to transfer in Atlanta twice? LISA I just think we should have paid the extra dollar-fifty and gotten a bus with restrooms. Homer wakes up, STRETCHES, and massages his knees. HOMER (SUDDENLY ALARMED) Marge, I can’t feel my legs! isan’ et feel my legs! He begins POUNDING on his legs. MARGE Homer, those legs belong to the man behind you.

Homer's pounding slows as he looks over his shoulder to see AN EXTREMELY TALL, VERY ANGRY MAN, who scowls at Homer.

LISA

(GASP) We're here!

SCENE 4 (CONT’D) They peer out the windows as skyscrapers loom up before them.

MARGE

HH rh a) oO fet

(IMPRESSED) Wow! (UPBEAT) like such a nobody. EXT. NEW YORK CITY - LINCOLN TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS The bus emerges from the tunnel into the city. A sign reads “Welcome to Manhattan -- Home of the World-Weary Poseur."

INT. BUS - CONTINUOUS

As Homer pouts, the rest of the family points out the sights.

MARGE (ENTHRALLED) It’s wall-to-wall landmarks -- Governor’s Island! The Williamsburg Bridge! Fourth Avenue!

Bart sees THREE BEARDED HASIDIC JEWS walking in the diamond district.

BART Look, it’s ZZ Top! (LEANS OUT WINDOW) You guys rock! HASIDIC MAN Eh, maybe a little. The bus pulls into the Port Authority bus terminal. EXT. TERMINAL - A LITTLE LATER

The Simpsons emerge from the station.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96 Page i4.

HOMER SCENE 4 (CONT’D) Now remember, criminals prey on small- town folk like us. So if anyone asks, we’re sophisticated millionaires from the Ozarks.

MARGE Homer, you're scaring the children.

HOMER No I’m not. I don’t want to spend one extra second in this urban death-maze. You wait here at the bus station while I get the car.

MARGE (ANNOYED) Homer, the bus station is just one of the sights we came to see. (FIRMLY) We’ll meet you in Central Park at five o'clock.

HOMER All right, but not a minute later -- ‘cause once the sun goes down, all the weirdos turn crazy.

As Homer steps into a waiting cab, he whirls toward an innocent BYSTANDER.

HOMER (CONT’D) I’m on to you! EXT. WORLD TRADE CENTER PLAZA - A LITTLE LATER

Homer pays the CAB DRIVER, takes out the letter from the Department of Motor Vehicles and reads it.

HOMER SCENE 4 (CONT’D) One World Trade Plaza. Hmmm... He walks up the steps, and sees the Twin Towers. HOMER (CONT’D) (IMPRESSED) Ooohh... Then he looks down. HOMER’S P.O.V.

Right between the towers, in the middle of the plaza, is Homer’s car.

HOMER (CONT’D) (SCREAMS) CLOSE-UP - WINDSHIELD It’s covered with dozens of tickets. HOMER (CONT’D) (SCREAMS LOUDER)

Homer turns to a sign that reads "No Screaming -- $50 Fine."

HOMER (CONT’D) (VERY QUIET SCREAM) EXT. STATUE OF LIBERTY - ESTABLISHING SHOT - DAY SCENE 5 EXT. LIBERTY ISLAND - NEAR STATUE PEDESTAL - DAY FOREIGN TOURISTS point and SNAP pictures. Bart, Lisa and Marge admire the statue.

BART SCENE 5 (CONT'D) There she is, Lis. America’s hood ornament . MARGE Well, let’s go inside and see what our tax dollars paid for. LISA Actually, Mom, it was built by the French. IMMIGRANTS SCENE 5 (CONT‘D) (DISAPPOINTED MOANS) EXT. CENTER OF WORLD TRADE CENTER PLAZA - DAY Annoyed PEDESTRIANS have to go around Homer’s car as they walk between the towers. Homer examines his wheel, which has a fluorescent orange Denver Boot on it. HOMER Stupid city! What the hell is this thing?

Homer YANKS on the boot and tries to loosen the nuts with his fingers, but nothing budges.

HOMER (CONT’D) Come off! Come off!

He bends down, BITES on a nut, and tries to turn it with his teeth.

HOMER (CONT’D) (MUFFLED PROFANITIES) (RUBS JAW) Ow. A MAN leans out the window of one of the towers. OFFICE WORKER IN TOWER ONE Hey, when you’re done with that, I got something up here you can bite on! OFFICE WORKER IN TOWER TWO Be polite, ya stinkin’ pus-bag! (TO HOMER) Ya gotta call that number on the boot. Homer turns and sees a phone number printed on the boot. OFFICE WORKER IN TOWER TWO (CONT’D) Sorry about that guy. They stick all

the jerks in Tower One.

INT. ANOTHER SUBWAY CAR - SIMULTANEOUS SCENE 6 (CONT’D)

Bart is standing in the middle of the car, RATTLING a small tin cup with "GIVE" written on it.

BART

Ladies and Gentlemen, I’m sorry to disturb your pleasant ride, but unlike yourselves, I was born without taste buds. het me demonstrate.

BART (CONT’D)

(DISGUSTED NOISE, DEFEATED) I’m over my head here. (TO PEOPLE) Thank you for your time.

EXT. CENTER OF WORLD TRADE CENTER PLAZA - A LITTLE LATER

Homer is now sitting on the ground next to the car. He checks his watch, which reads 11:05.

HOMER (WHINY MOAN) (THEN, YELLING AT WATCH) ° Come on! Pick up the pace!

A quarter falls into Homer’s lap. He looks up to see that a few PASSERSBY are tossing coins to him.

HOMER (CONT’D) Hey! I’m not a bum... (COIN LANDS) Thanks, but I don’t need... (COIN LANDS) Okay, if you insist, but... (COIN LANDS) Spare some change, lady? (NO COIN) (BITTERLY) Lousy

cheapskate...

SCENE 6 (CONT'D) Homer counts the change in his hand, then gets an idea. He looks up to see a pizza parlor across the street. The sign reads "ORIGINAL FAMOUS RAY’S -- not affiliated with Famous Original Ray's." HOMER (CONT’D) So hungry... can’t leave car...

Homer puts one foot on the bumper and tries to stretch himself across the street to the pizza parlor.

HOMER (CONT’D) (STRETCHING GRUNTS) Getting closer... closer... He picks up a stick and reaches with it. HOMER (CONT’D) ...almost there... (BONE POPPING NOISE) Ow!

He gives up and rubs his shoulder. A FOOD VENDOR approaches.

VENDOR (GRUFF MIDDLE EASTERN ACCENT) Fresh Khlav Kalash! Get your Khlav Kalash! HOMER Could you go across the street and get me a slice of pizza? VENDOR No pizza. Only Khlav Kalash. HOMER (RESIGNED) All right, all right. Give

me one bowl.

He hands Homer a huge clump of food on a stick. Homer TAKES A BITE and grimaces.

Homer LICKS the stick clean, then LICKS OFF each of his fingers.

Marge, Lisa and Bart admire the pagodas, Chinese writing, etc.

MARGE Are we still in Little Italy? LISA Actually, I think. we’re in Chinatown now. MARGE (AMAZED) Only in New York!

They pass a Chinese grocery store, with skinned rabbits hanging in the window.

EXT, WORLD TRADE CENTER PLAZA ~ DAY Homer is sitting on his car with his legs crossed, rocking back and forth. He tries to twist his legs even further around each other. HOMER (PLEADING) Come on, boot guy!

A BUS GOES BY displaying its destination: "FLUSHING MEADOWS." Homer looks at it wistfully.

HOMER’S FANTASY

Homer wanders happily across a giant green meadow covered with toilets. We hear CHOIR MUSIC and toilets FLUSHING.

BACK TO REALITY HOMER I can’t take it anymore. (TO CAR) I’‘ll only be gone for a second. He begins backing slowly toward the tower, keeping an eye on the car, then speeds up and whirls around to run as he enters the building. INT. Frontsies... Backsies... I’m with

them... They gave me frontsies...

SCENE 7 (CONT’D) Homer buys his ticket and runs to the elevator. He takes one last look at his car, then shoves a SOCIETY MATRON out of the way and takes the last space in the elevator. The DOORS CLOSE behind him. SOCIETY MATRON How frightfully rude. I certainly hope someone stabs him in the eye. INT. OBSERVATION DECK - A MINUTE LATER The elevator DOORS OPEN and Homer DASHES out to the restroom. It is padlocked and a sign reads "OUT OF ORDER ~- USE OTHER TOWER." HOMER (PAINED MOAN) He ZIPS his pants up and turns around. EXT. WORLD TRADE CENTER TOWER - CONTINUOUS In a WIDE SHOT, we PAN SLOWLY DOWN the tower to the base. A tiny Homer emerges, runs across to the other tower, and we PAN BACK UP. After a beat we hear... HOMER (0.S.) (EXAGGERATED) AAAAAAHHHHHHHHH . INT. MEN’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS

As Homer stands at the urinal, he looks out a window to the street below.

HOMER'S P.O.V. He sees a UNIFORMED OFFICER standing by the car, holding a boot-removal wrench and looking at his watch impatiently. He writes a ticket and adds it to the pile. HOMER (LOUD ANNOYED GRUNT)

As the officer drives off, Homer’s ANNOYED GRUNT ECHOES through the city.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/

SCENE 7 (CONT'D) EXT. TOP OF EMPIRE STATE BUILDING - CONTINUOUS

Lisa hears this and reacts. LISA Bart, that sounds like Dad! Maybe he’s in trouble!

BART

(HOLDING SOFT PRETZEL) Hey, I’m eatin’

here!

FADE OUT:

END OF ACT. TWO

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

ACT THREE FADE IN: SCENE 8 EXT. WORLD TRADE CENTER PLAZA - A MINUTE LATER

Homer, GASPING for breath, runs out of the tower to his car. He picks up the new ticket and reads it.

HOMER (READING) "Failure to wait by vehicle: $250." (ANGRY MUTTERING)

The sun starts to go down behind the towers, throwing a dark shadow over Homer and the car.

HOMER (CONT’D)

Oh no, it’s getting dark. (DETERMINED)

Well, that’s it. I’m getting out of

this town alive if it kills me! Homer sweeps the tickets off the windshield, gets in the car, STARTS IT UP, and STEPS ON THE GAS. The car doesn’t budge. CLOSE-UP - FRONT WHEEL The wheel STRAINS, but the boot prevents it from turning. ANGLE ON HOMER

With a look of grim determination, Homer FLOORS IT. Smoke billows from the engine.

CLOSE-UP - FRONT WHEEL

The wheel finally begins to turn. The boot LIFTS the car, and slowly tears through the fender with a horrendous SCREECHING of metal. The car limps forward, rising and falling as it LURCHES along at one mile an hour.

ANGLE ON HOMER

With each THUMP, his head BANGS against the ceiling.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

HOMER (CONT'D) SCENE 8 (CONT’D) (SNEAKY CHUCKLE) Ow! (SNEAKY CHUCKLE) Ow! (SILENT BEAT) Ow!

The car CLUNKS down the steps, gouging holes in the plaza. Homer puts on his turn signal and merges into traffic.

EXT. SIDEWALK - MIDTOWN - AFTERNOON

Marge, Lisa, and Bart are looking in upscale store windows.

Marge gawks at a chic display of fancy women’s shoes. LISA Look at all proea beautiful shoes! I know they’re made from animals, but wow! MARGE (WISTFUL) If only I didn’t already have a pair of shoes. BART Speaking of shoes, I don’t care about shoes. I’11 meet you ladies back here in half an hour. LISA (MESMERIZED BY SHOES) Okay, Dad... EXT. ACROSS THE STREET - A MOMENT LATER

Bart wanders down the street, taking in the sights. Suddenly his eyes widen and he GASPS.

BART’S POV

His eyes have locked on a small plaque which reads "MAD Magazine... 6th Floor." He charges inside.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

INT. OFFICE BUILDING HALLWAY - DAY SCENE 8 (CONT’D) An elevator DINGS and Bart steps out. An arrow points left to "MAD Magazine." Another points right to: "Methadone Clinic." Bart heads left, EVERYONE else goes right. Bart takes a deep BREATH, OPENS the door, and sees... INT. "MAD® RECEPTION AREA - CONTINUOUS A small drab reception room where a sour-looking RECEPTIONIST sits at a desk. A large sign behind her reads "MAD." BART Excuse me, is this MAD Magazine? RECEPTIONIST (SNAPPY) No, it’s "Mademoiselle." We’re buying our sign on the installment plan. BART Seriously, though -- my name is Bart Simpson. My father has a subscription. I’d like the grand tour, please. RECEPTIONIST Listen, kid. You probably think lots of crazy stuff goes on in there (GESTURES AT DOOR), but this is just a place of business. BART (DISAPPOINTED) Oh. Okay.

Just then the door opens behind the receptionist and ALFRED E. NEUMAN leans out.

ALFRED E. NEUMAN SCENE 8 (CONT'D) Get me Kaputnik and Fonebone! I wanna see their drawings for "New Kids on the Blecch". Neuman heads back inside, revealing a glimpse of the activities within: the MAD zeppelin flying by, a UNICYCLIST in a propeller beanie chasing the zeppelin with a butterfly net, and a DON MARTIN-ESQUE GUY on a pogo stick bouncing around with the sound effect: POIT, POIT, POIT. Bart’s jaw drops in wonder. ALFRED E. NEUMAN (CONT’D) And where are my furshlugginer pastrami sandwiches?! Neuman CLOSES the door. BART (AWED) That was the single greatest moment of my or anyone else’s life.

EXT. TIFFANY’S - DAY

We see a CLOSE-UP of the sign, then PAN DOWN to Marge and Lisa at the impressive entrance.

MARGE The important thing is to set a budget

before we go in. Twenty dollars each.

Agreed? LISA Agreed. DISSOLVE TO: EXT. TIFFANY’S - A LITTLE LATER SCENE 9

Marge and Lisa exit happily, carrying Tiffany’s bags.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

MARGE SCENE 9 (CONT’D) Well, we went a little over our budget, but it was worth it! LISA Let’s look at them again! They each take Tiffany boxes from their bags and admire them. They open the boxes and we see there’s nothing inside. MARGE (PROUD) Look how the lid slides up and down. I’m going to put mine on the mantlepiece. LISA Take a picture of me with my box, Mom! Then when we get home, I’1l keep the picture in the box. MARGE (IMPRESSED) Ooh, I better make it a good one. Marge SNAPS a picture. We FLASH TO A FREEZE-FRAME of the photo: the box looks beautiful, but all of Lisa’s head is cut off except for her happy smile. EXT. MIDTOWN AVENUE - DAY Homer’s car slowly THUMPS down the street, forcing angry, HONKING MOTORISTS to go around him. Each time the boot comes down, it GOUGES a pothole and sends an asphalt divot flying into the cars behind. (Steam shoots out of some of the potholes.) A cab PULLS UP beside him. ANGRY CAB DRIVER

Get off the road, ya freakin’ maniac!

The cab SPEEDS BY. Under the rear bumper, we see @ MESSENGER on a mangled bicycle being DRAGGED along.

ve

BIKE MESSENGER SCENE 9 (CONT'D) (SQUEAKY VOICE, TO HOMER) Yeah, ya jackass! ANGLE ON HOMER

He grips the wheel tightly, a look of grim rage on his face.

HOMER Shut up! Shut up! He CLICKS ON THE RADIO to drown out the honking and abuse. SONG ON RADIO "Everything is beautiful / In its own way..." Homer KICKS the radio and destroys it. EXT. TIMES SQUARE - SIMULTANEOUS Marge and the kids gaze up at a theater marquee. (Around Times Square, we see other theater marquees that read "Tommy Tune in GOTTA MINCE!", "Ernest Goes to Broadway", "Neil Simon’s MORE ABOUT BROOKLYN", "Hoofin’ ‘N’ Mouthin’", and "David Copperfield’s Astonishing Girlfriend". ) LISA (READING) "Kickin’ It! A Musical Journey Through the Betty Ford Center." MARGE You know, when I was a girl, I always dreamed of being in a Broadway audience. They go to the ticket window. INT. stage. MR. CLEARY, a nervous young Robert Downey, eben type, stands before a stern JUDGE, who addresses the JURY.

JUDGE SCENE 9 (CONT’D) How do you find the defendant? JUDGE I should put you away / Where you can’t kill or maim us / But this is L.A. / (SUDDEN TENOR) And you’re rich and fa- mous... EXT. MIDTOWN STREET - DAY SCENE 10

Homer’s car CLOMPS uptown, the ENGINE STRUGGLING and smoking. The sun is very low on the horizon.

HOMER (MOANS) It’s gonna be dark soon. I’1l never make it out of here with this rotten boot on my car.

Homer sees he’s approaching a massive road construction site. He SCREECHES to a halt.

HOMER (CONT’D) (GETTING AN IDEA) Aha! I’ve got it! Brain, how can I ever thank you? HOMER'S BRAIN (V.0.) Just don’t bump me on your way out of the car.

Homer leaps out of the car, BANGING his head against the door frame on the way out.

HOMER (MEEKLY) Sorry. ANOTHER ANGLE In an OVERHEAD SHOT, we see that the construction is blocking off every lane but one, which Homer’s car is now obstructing. Many DRIVERS begin HONKING and SHOUTING. We PULL BACK TO REVEAL that traffic is at a standstill for blocks behind him. HOMER (CONT’D) (TO OTHER DRIVERS, REASSURING) Just 4 minute... Everything’s under control...

I’d be upset, too...

Homer approaches a CONSTRUCTION WORKER operating a JACKHAMMER .

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

HOMER (CONT’D) SCENE 10 (CONT’D) Hey, the boss says you're fired. CONSTRUCTION WORKER (ROLLING UP SLEEVE) I’1ll get him! The construction worker runs off. HOMER (SNEAKY CHUCKLE) Homer grabs the jackhammer and runs back to his car. As the furious HONKING intensifies, Homer presses the jackhammer against the boot. HOMER (CONT’D) So long, Mr. Boot. He TURNS ON THE JACKHAMMER, and starts POUNDING away at the boot. He immediately loses control of it and HAMMERS a line of deep dents into the car before steering it back to the boot. The car VIBRATES furiously. HOMER (CONT’D) (VIBRATING) Come o-o-on! Come o-o-on! One by one, the car windows, headlights, mirrors, and windshields SHATTER. The hubcaps and bumpers FALL OFF, the AIRBAG DEPLOYS. The hood POPS OPEN and bolts unscrew from the engine. Finally, the boot POPS OFF. Homer continues jackhammering it out of spite, then DUSTS his hands. HOMER (CONT'D)

Homer one, New York nothing.

Homer takes one step and we hear a loud CLANK. He looks down to see the boot has clamped onto his foot.

HOMER (CONT’D) (ANNOYED GRUNT) He DRAGS it for a couple of difficult steps, then furiously knocks it off by BANGING it against the car several times.

He SLAMS the hood closed, OPENS the driver’s door, and turns to the traffic behind him.

HOMER (CONT’D) SCENE 10 (CONT’D) (CHEERY) Thanks for your patience!

A bullet WHIZZES by Homer’s head. He SCREAMS, leaps in the car, and SPEEDS OFF, the axles WOBBLING beneath him.

EXT. CENTRAL PARK - DUSK

Marge and the kids ride in a carriage as the HORSE CLOPS peacefully through the quiet park.

LISA What a perfect way to end our day in New York.

MARGE Now I know why they call it "The Big Apple" -- ‘cause you never know how much fun you’re going to have.

The CARRIAGE DRIVER gives Marge a bemused look over his shoulder. From offscreen, we hear a car HONKING furiously.

MARGE (CONT’D)

That’s the first rude New Yorker we’ve

come across. EXT. CENTRAL PARK - NOT FAR AWAY - SIMULTANEOUS The HONKING is coming from Homer’s car. He cuts through the middle of a picnic, CRUSHING the picnic basket and sending PEOPLE SCATTERING.

PICNICKERS (SCREAM)

Homer leans out the window and turns back toward the picnickers. HOMER Comin’ through!

Only a sliver of sun remains. The street lights flicker on.

HOMER (CONT'D) SCENE 10 (CONT’D) (ALARMED) No, no! I refuse to be trapped here after dark!

Homer ACCELERATES, SWERVING wildly through a basketball court.

HOMER (CONT’D) Run for your lives! You’re not safe here!

ANGLE ON HORSE-DRAWN CARRIAGE

CARRIAGE DRIVER My God, that maniac is heading straight for us! (STARTS WHIPPING HORSE) Go Secretariat! Go!

The carriage SPEEDS up. Homer pulls up alongside and BUMPS it. The driver turns and CRACKS his whip in Homer’s face.

HOMER Ow!

BART Hey, it’s Dad! Right on time!

HOMER We're getting outta here, now! Jump in, Marge! Trust me! Throw the kids! No time for the baby!

MARGE (FIRMLY) We’re not jumping, Homer.

Homer SPEEDS ahead and FISHTAILS the car, blocking in the

carriage. He leaps out and begins hustling the family into the car.

@ 39.

4F22 RECORD 11/4/96

HOMER SCENE 10 (CONT’D) Come on! Let’s go! Everyone into the car! Now! He shoos them in and SPEEDS AWAY. After a second, the car SCREECHES to a quick stop, the DOOR FLIES OPEN and the carriage driver is SHOVED out. CARRIAGE DRIVER (TIPPING HAT, CHEERY) Tell your friends to ask for Jimmy. The Simpsons SPEED AWAY. “EXT. GEORGE WASHINGTON BRIDGE - A FEW MINUTES LATER The car is on the bridge heading out of the city. Marge and the kids look longingly out the window at the sparkling Manhattan skyline. LISA What a magical city! Can we come back next year, Dad? We PAN OVER to Homer, who remains fixated on the road ahead. Pieces of trash BLOWING OFF a garbage truck in front of him STRIKE him in the face (through the shattered windshield) . HOMER (CALM) We'll see, honey.

A bag of medical waste HITS him in the chest and BREAKS OPEN.

HOMER (CONT'D) (CALMER) We'll see. MUSIC: "NEW YORK, NEW YORK" FADE OUT:

THE END

Page 40.
